scene: I took the child by night
title: Joseph: I Took the Child by Night
narration: We were going home. Toward Nazareth.
The danger had only begun to spread its shadow over the Child.
Then sleep took me, and in the sleep an angel.
Rise. Take the Boy and his Mother. Flee into Egypt.
Herod will seek the Child to destroy him.
I wake. My heart still pounding with the word.
I shake her awake. I tell her everything I heard.
No delay. None. She rises at once.
Her whole being trembles at the voice.
She will not be found careless of her Son's life.
She lifts the sleeping Boy, and he cries, startled, untimely.
I watch them, and something in me breaks with tenderness.
It is night. We go out into the dark.
By night, because flight belongs to night more than to day.
So the thing be done in secret, the danger slipped past unseen.
We had the word of God — yet still we use the human road.
We do not omit what we ourselves can do.
The flight is hard. The darkness makes it harder.
She is a girl, lately risen from childbirth, delicate.
I am old. The roads are rough and strange to us.
And the Child too small, too tender, to carry such a way.
Poor, with almost nothing, into a foreign land we do not know.
Among idolaters we are forced to dwell.
All of this is the matter of our affliction.
Yet I tell myself: this God grants no privilege he kept from himself.
Nor from her. So I take patience, and I walk.
Egypt — that he might give light to those who sit in darkness.
The land that burned with impiety, he will kindle with the fire of faith.
prayer: who while still an infant willed to suffer persecution and exile,
give me, wretched as I am,
to bear all these things patiently for you —
and even death, if it must be.
Teach me to despise all the world's prosperity
and to fear none of its adversity.
When consolation comes, let me not be lifted up;
when trouble comes, let me not be broken.
Let me walk, like Joseph, by night if I must,
trusting the word that wakes me. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Surge et accipe Puerum et Matrem ejus, et fuge in Egyptum.
latinJewel.english: Rise and take the Child and his Mother, and flee into Egypt.
